finding: A long turn ENDING on a structural node renders completely unclamped with no toggle: splitTrailingProse returns kinds.length, clampedNodes is empty, and the guard falls through to the plain render. Reproduced on the gallery surface: 1044px tall, no collapsible, no toggle, where it previously clamped to 384px. Found independently by two auditors.
resolution: fixed — ITEM-3 reverted in full (DRIFT-2.1); ChatMessage.tsx restored to base

finding: The collapse DECISION and the collapse SCOPE measure different things. offerCollapse comes from messageText(message).length over ALL text blocks, but only the trailing prose run is placed under the clamp. For [text 5000] -> [tool_use] -> [text 100] the clamp wraps ~100 chars, measures no overflow, and no toggle renders while the 5000-char prefix shows at full height.
resolution: fixed — ITEM-3 reverted; the clamp again holds the whole bubble so decision and scope coincide

finding: Virtualizer estimate error flips sign for tool-using turns. computeEstimate is clamp-unaware; previously a clamped turn was a bounded OVER-estimate, but with the split it becomes an unbounded UNDER-estimate — the direction that makes getTotalSize() grow as rows scroll in and the scrollbar thumb jump.
resolution: fixed — moot once ITEM-3 was reverted; real heights are clamped again as before

finding: TEST-8's card assertion was vacuous: post-split, structural cards were never children of the collapsible in ANY state, so `filter(insideClamp).toHaveLength(0)` was state-independent and merely restated TEST-3. It also lacked a non-emptiness guard, so it would pass on a surface rendering zero cards.
resolution: fixed — spec rewritten; TEST-8 now asserts expansion-specific properties plus ring room, and expectRingRoom() requires >=3 cards inside the clamp

finding: TEST-2 claimed 'interleaved order preserved' but the order array was built only from card elements and the collapsible container — TEXT nodes were excluded, so a genuine reorder of the mid-turn narration was undetectable. That was the property the fixture's design comment leaned on most.
resolution: fixed — the order signature now includes prose ('card' | 'text') and asserts a text block precedes the last card

finding: Theme looping was coverage inflation: every assertion in the looped tests was structural/geometric and none read a color, so light and dark exercised identical logic. Meanwhile the actual user-facing symptom (a washed-out ring) was never asserted in any theme.
resolution: fixed — the looped tests now assert the ring-room condition that determines ring visibility, which is what differs per theme (ring-foreground/10 resolves differently); the pixel proof per theme is recorded in REPRO.md

finding: The `clampedNodes.length > 0` guard silently disabled collapsing for a long turn ending on a structural node, and nothing — unit, e2e, or fixture — pinned that consequence at the component level.
resolution: fixed — the guard is gone with the revert, and TEST-5 now explicitly pins height-bounding on this surface (DRIFT-2.5)

finding: readTurn used a GLOBAL document.querySelector for collapsible-content, which is not unique per page. If the fixture ever gains an earlier clamping message it would silently measure the wrong turn while still appearing to assert meaningfully.
resolution: fixed — readTurn now scopes to the assistant chat-message that actually contains a collapsible, and throws a named error if none exists

finding: TEST-4 asserted the fix's MECHANISM (paddingLeft==='2px', marginLeft==='-2px') rather than its EFFECT, so any equivalent re-implementation (wrapper element, mask-clip override) would fail spuriously while the surface stayed correct — and such a test would not have caught the original bug.
resolution: fixed — TEST-3 now asserts ring ROOM (>=1px between card border box and clip edge), and TEST-4 asserts the no-reflow effect instead

finding: The inset's correctness depends on an unenforced cross-file invariant: CollapsibleBlock is a generic exported component but is only correct when mounted inside a parent supplying >=2px horizontal padding (today ChatMessage's px-0.5). Nothing asserts the parent's padding, so removing it would silently reintroduce the shaved ring.
resolution: mitigated — the component comment states the requirement explicitly, and the spec asserts the ring-room EFFECT, so a parent that stops absorbing the 4px fails the test regardless of mechanism

finding: The inset is HORIZONTAL only. A bordered prose-level element that is the FIRST child of the clamped region (a markdown table or code fence opening the content) still has its TOP hairline clipped by overflow-hidden, so the TEST-4 rationale about protecting prose-level bordered content overstated the coverage.
resolution: accepted, not actioned — pre-existing and out of scope for #183; adding -my/py would change the clamp's vertical geometry and the scrollHeight overflow measurement. Recorded in DRIFT-2.7 as a follow-up; the spec no longer claims that coverage.

finding: Verified CORRECT with zero slack: the content div is a stretched flex item, so -2px margins widen its border box to exactly the parent's padding box, and 2px padding restores the content box. A child Card's ring-1 then paints 1px inside BOTH clips. Any future ring-2 or 2px focus ring on prose-level content inside the clamp would still be shaved.
resolution: acknowledged — measured empirically at 390/768/1280 in both themes: no horizontal page scroll, zero clipped cards, card width unchanged at 860px

finding: RTL hypothesis does not hold: -mx-/px- are symmetric and therefore direction-neutral, and the logical-direction lint's rule table matches only pl-/pr-/ml-/mr-/left-/right-/text-left/text-right. No violation.
resolution: no action — confirmed independently by `npm run lint:logical-direction` passing on the changed code

finding: STRUCTURAL_CONTENT_TYPES was a hardcoded string Set with no link to the renderers that actually draw cards, and content_type is typed `string`, so classifyNode failed OPEN — an unrecognized type would be classified prose and land inside the clamp, silently reintroducing the bug for a future card-rendering block type.
resolution: fixed — moot; the helper is removed entirely with the ITEM-3 revert

finding: Slicing bubbleNodes into two separately-parented lists means a node that migrates between parents across re-renders is reconciled as unmount+remount, losing unpersisted local state — e.g. ThinkingContent's useState(isExpanded) would snap shut. Low reachability (needs a post-mount content mutation on an already-rendered history message).
resolution: fixed — moot with the ITEM-3 revert; there is only one list again

finding: The ResizeObserver/measure() target changed meaning without the component changing: contentRef wrapped only the trailing prose, so `overflowing` — which gates both the toggle and data-collapsed — was derived from a strictly smaller element. data-collapsed could vanish for long messages, and a persisted collapsed[messageId] entry could be stranded when CollapsibleBlock never mounts.
resolution: fixed — moot with the ITEM-3 revert; the clamp again measures the whole bubble

finding: Split-specific spacing seam: the hoisted prefix and the CollapsibleBlock were siblings under a parent with no gap, so the last card and the clamped prose lost the 8px separation every other adjacent pair keeps. Otherwise the aria relationship and focus-auto-expand path were unaffected (arguably improved).
resolution: fixed — moot with the ITEM-3 revert; all blocks share one gap-2 container again

finding: Per-node classifyNode work is O(n) total over disjoint slices, not O(n^2), and is dwarfed by the React element construction already happening in the same loop. No meaningful cost.
resolution: no action — and moot with the ITEM-3 revert

finding: Checked the new deep-state bundle against its sibling `toolGroup`: same message()/block() typed builders, same synthetic dee9000N id scheme, registered in both chatDeepById and CHAT_DEEP_CONVERSATION_IDS, and the gallery entry follows the deep-chat-tool-group shape with an interactions recipe modelled on deep-chat-mcp-toolcall-error. No divergence from the established pattern; the fixture is synthetic so gallery:check-fixtures (which guards recorded ids) is unaffected.
resolution: no action — conformance verified, no defect

finding: CollapsibleBlock is a generic exported component whose correctness now carries a requirement that cannot be expressed through its props: the parent must supply >=2px horizontal padding to absorb the inset's 4px of growth. A caller reading the prop signature (children, maxHeightPx, messageId, className) has no way to discover this, so a second consumer could mount it correctly by the types and still get a shaved ring.
resolution: mitigated rather than re-architected — there is exactly ONE consumer today (ChatMessage), the requirement is stated in the component comment, and the spec asserts the ring-room EFFECT so a violating parent fails the test. A prop-level contract (e.g. insetPx) would be the right move if a second consumer ever appears; noted rather than speculatively generalised.

finding: ROUND 2: the inset was HORIZONTAL only, but the clip applies on all four sides. The first child card's border-box top is flush with the clamp's top, so its 1px top ring was still erased by both overflow-hidden and mask-clip:border-box. Reproduced by measurement: thinking-card topRing delta 0 while collapsed vs 25 expanded — the same issue-183 symptom, unfixed for the top edge.
resolution: fixed — inset widened to all four sides (-m-0.5 p-0.5). Re-measured: every card now reads 25/25 on left and top in both states. Vertical overhang is safe because the ancestor uses overflow-x:clip specifically so overflow-y stays visible.

finding: ROUND 2: the code comment claimed 'the regression spec asserts the ring's VISIBILITY, not these classes, so a parent that stops absorbing it fails the test'. That was FALSE — expectRingRoom measured roomLeft/roomRight against the CLAMP's own client rect, never against the clipping ancestor, so deleting ChatMessage's px-0.5 would re-clip the rings with every test still green. The comment actively misled the next maintainer.
resolution: fixed both ways — readTurn now walks up to the NEAREST CLIPPING ANCESTOR (first ancestor with a non-visible overflow or a mask) and measures against it, and the comment no longer claims protection it cannot deliver; it now states plainly that the cross-file invariant must be re-verified by hand.

finding: ROUND 2: expectRingRoom checked only left/right and CardBox carried no top/bottom gap, so the top-edge clipping defect above was structurally invisible to the suite — the first card could ship with no top border and all tests pass.
resolution: fixed — expectRingRoom now asserts all four sides; verified to catch the horizontal-only inset with 'thinking-card: only 0px between the card and its clipping ancestor (collapsible-content) on TOP'.

finding: ROUND 2: the spec was pure layout geometry and never verified a ring is PAINTED. Setting the kit Card to ring-0 would erase every border in every state while TEST-3 ('every card's ring renders while COLLAPSED') still passed — the name asserted rendering, the code asserted only room for it.
resolution: fixed — added isEdgePainted(), which screenshots a 2px strip covering the ring pixel plus 1px of background and compares it byte-wise against an identical strip of bare background (no image-decode dependency). Verified against a temporary ring-0 edit: fails in BOTH themes.

finding: ROUND 2 (self-found while validating the above): the first isEdgePainted implementation used a 5px window spanning the card's INTERIOR, so in dark mode bg-card differs from the page background and the strip differed regardless of the ring — a ring-0 regression passed in dark while failing in light. It also added window.scrollX/Y to the clip, but Playwright's clip for a viewport screenshot is already in viewport coordinates, so every border read as missing.
resolution: fixed — window narrowed to exactly the ring pixel + 1px of background (interior excluded) and the scroll offset removed. Re-verified: ring-0 now fails in BOTH themes, and the real code passes in both.

finding: ROUND 2: TEST-2's ramp coverage was half tautology — `inside.some(c => c.topRelClamp < ramp)` is satisfied by the first card, always at topRelClamp ~0, so it can never fail. Only the bottomRelClamp half carried information.
resolution: fixed — both halves now assert on bottomRelClamp (one card entirely above the ramp, one reaching past it), plus a new assertion that a card sits flush at the clamp's top, which is the position the top-edge defect needed.

finding: ROUND 2: TEST-8's expectRingRoom call was vacuous — the test had just asserted maskImage==='none' and overflow!=='hidden', so there is no clip and 'room inside the clip edge' has no bearing on whether the ring paints. The 'and still crisp' claim was untested.
resolution: fixed — TEST-8 now asserts PAINT (isEdgePainted) rather than room, which is the only meaningful check in an unclipped state.

finding: ROUND 2: the order assertion `indexOf('text') < lastIndexOf('card')` was far weaker than its prose 'interleaved order preserved' — it fails only if ALL prose sits after the last card, so the card/text/card/text interleave the fixture depends on was never verified.
resolution: fixed — now asserts the exact leading pattern ['card','text','card','text','card','text'].

finding: ROUND 2: openSurface's scrollIntoView comment carried a stale rationale about pixel sampling from an earlier draft, misrepresenting what the helper protects.
resolution: fixed — comment rewritten; and the rationale is now true again, since the paint checks do screenshot real pixels and require the cards on screen.

finding: ROUND 2: the mask ramp still fades rings in the 288-384px band, which is literally the 'washed out border' wording of issue #183, so the fix could be called incomplete for a card low in the clamp.
resolution: accepted by design, not a defect — the ramp is the intended 'there is more below' cue and it fades ALL content there (text included), not just rings. Measured attenuation at the ramp start is ~4% (delta 24 vs 25). Removing it would reintroduce the color-band problem the mask exists to avoid. The reported defect was the hard clip, which erased rings entirely at ANY position including the very top.

finding: ROUND 2: checked each named regression path against the four-sided inset — scrollHeight>maxHeightPx+1 overflow measurement, streaming re-measure, RTL, useInPlaceAnchor anchoring, short messages, the w-fit user bubble. No defect found; the content box is unchanged so wrapping and measurement are identical, and mx/px are direction-symmetric.
resolution: no action — corroborated by the 14 passing visual/scroll-stability specs (incl. corrections<=2, in-place anchor, show-more persistence) and the backend e2e.

finding: ROUND 3: TEST-4 could not detect what its comment claimed. Because -m-0.5 p-0.5 cancels exactly, a state-GATED inset yields the same content-box width collapsed and expanded, so comparing card widths across states passes on the very regression it claimed to guard. The underlying rationale in the component comment ('gating it would reflow the message on every toggle') was therefore also false.
resolution: fixed — TEST-4 now asserts the inset SELF-CANCELS (clamped container's content width == its parent's), which catches the realistic mistake of writing p-0.5 without -m-0.5. The component comment no longer claims a reflow rationale it cannot support.

finding: ROUND 3: expectRingRoom demanded roomBottom >= 1 for every card inside the clamp, but the clamp's bottom edge IS the fold — a card bisected by it legitimately has a negative bottom gap. One extra line of fixture narration, or a slightly different default font, would push the last card across 384px and raise a false 'This is issue #183' failure on a correctly-rendering surface.
resolution: fixed — roomBottom removed from the assertion set, with the reasoning recorded inline. Left/right/top are asserted; the bottom fold is intended behaviour.

finding: ROUND 3: isEdgePainted failed OPEN (`return true`) on any negative coordinate, while openSurface's scrollIntoView({block:'start'}) drives the turn toward y=0 — maximising the chance the guard trips. A ~10px chrome-height change would silently vacate the only assertion covering the vertical half of the fix.
resolution: fixed — now throws an explicit error naming the card and its position, so an unmeasurable sample fails loudly instead of reporting a pass.

finding: ROUND 3: the roomLeft/roomRight doc comment claimed that measuring against the nearest clipping ancestor is what catches removal of ChatMessage's px-0.5. False — while collapsed the nearest clipper is the clamped container itself, whose own 2px padding reports comfortable room regardless of any ancestor. The cross-file invariant was covered only incidentally by the paint check.
resolution: fixed — readTurn now takes the TIGHTEST gap across EVERY clipping ancestor within the message, tracked PER AXIS (the bubble's clip layer is overflow-x:clip with overflow-y:visible, so it constrains sides but not top). Verified by deleting px-0.5: TEST-3 fails with 'only 0px … on LEFT'.

finding: ROUND 3: the 'leaves the CONTENT box byte-identical' claim holds for width but not for the clamp. With box-sizing: border-box, max-height:384 bounds the border box including the new 4px of vertical padding, so visible content is 380px, not 384. Self-consistent (scrollHeight gains the same padding) but the comment overstated it.
resolution: fixed — comment corrected to state the width claim precisely and to call out the vertical trade-off (380px visible) plus the consequence of scaling the inset up.

finding: ROUND 3: the content div's -mb-0.5 is not margin-collapsed in a flex column, so the Show more/less toggle's mt-1 yielded an effective 2px gap — half the previous value and off the 4px design rhythm.
resolution: fixed independently before this audit round landed — toggle bumped to mt-1.5 (6px - 2px = 4px) with the coupling to the inset value documented inline. Re-measured: toggleGap back to 4px.

finding: ROUND 3 (self-found while fixing the above): taking the tightest gap across ALL clippers without distinguishing axes produced a FALSE failure — the bubble's clip layer is overflow-x:clip / overflow-y:visible, so measuring a vertical gap against it reported roomTop 0 for an element it does not vertically clip. An earlier attempt that walked past the message root was also wrong, picking up the message-list scroll container.
resolution: fixed — clipper detection is per-axis and the walk stops at the message root. Diagnosed by dumping the actual clipper chain rather than guessing; all 7 specs pass and the px-0.5 deletion is still caught.

finding: ROUND 4: isEdgePainted had no fold/mask-ramp guard, contradicting the concession expectRingRoom makes 25 lines above. The mask is linear-gradient(black 75%, transparent), so a card whose sample point drifts into the ramp has its ring alpha multiplied toward 0 (the 25/255 delta can round to byte-identical), and one pushed past the fold samples clipped background in both strips — either reports 'not painted' and blames issue #183 for benign fixture drift.
resolution: fixed — the paint checks now run only on cards whose sampled mid-height is ABOVE the ramp start, with a >=2 guard so the restriction cannot empty the set. Same reasoning as the roomBottom exclusion, now applied consistently.

finding: ROUND 4: the inset eroded the overflow probe's tolerance 4x. Under border-box sizing the 4px of vertical padding came out of the 384px clamp while scrollHeight gained the same 4px, moving the trigger from contentHeight>385 to >381 — messages 381-385px tall would clamp where they previously rendered untouched, showing a 'Show more' that reveals 1-4px.
resolution: fixed — introduced INSET_PX and derived clampBoxPx = maxHeightPx + INSET_PX*2, used for BOTH the max-height and the probe. Measured: visible content back to exactly 384px (was 380), margin box still 384px, probe tolerance restored. The two magic numbers are now coupled through one constant.

finding: ROUND 4: vertical spacing was compensated on the bottom only — margin-top:-2px placed the content 2px above the root's content-box top and shortened the block, with the toggle's mt-1.5 restoring only the bottom 2px.
resolution: fixed as a side effect of the clampBoxPx compensation — the border box is now 388px with -2/-2 margins, so the MARGIN box occupies exactly 384px (identical to the pre-fix layout) and the content's position inside is unchanged (border-top -2 + padding-top +2 = 0). Verified: contentAreaHeight 384, toggleGap 4.

finding: ROUND 4: `tightest` clipper tracking mixed axes — the y-branch overwrote an x-branch winner without comparing magnitudes, so a LEFT failure message could name an element that is not the LEFT-tightest clipper, sending a debugger to the wrong file.
resolution: fixed — tracked separately as tightestX / tightestY and the failure message now names the axis-appropriate one ('its tightest TOP-clipping ancestor'). Verified in the injected-defect run.

finding: ROUND 4: clipper room was measured against getBoundingClientRect (the BORDER box) while an overflow clip cuts at the PADDING box. Latent today (no bordered clipper in the chain) but allClippers walks arbitrary ancestors, so a clipping ancestor that gained a border would report room that does not physically exist.
resolution: fixed — clientLeft/clientTop are now subtracted, making the helper as general as its walk.

finding: ROUND 4 (self-found): a gate:ui run reported 5 surfaces with thousands of HIGH findings — none of them the surface under test. Cause was my own concurrent source edits hot-reloading into the dev server mid-sweep, not a real regression.
resolution: no action on the code — re-run cleanly with no edits in flight. Recorded because a contaminated gate run is indistinguishable from a real failure at first glance and would otherwise be chased as a defect.
